# Audit importable Memory Core path fallbacks against AiConfig

## Context

This ticket is the deliverable importable-consumer slice of the broader ADR 0019 A1 cleanup under Epic #12456. The original audit-wide wording was too broad for one PR and duplicated parent-epic scope; after V-B-A against current source and the closed #12464 micro-split correction, this issue is scoped to the load-bearing Memory Core graph / wake path consumers that can read existing config leaves directly.

Authority: ADR 0019 â€” `AiConfig` and child providers are the reactive Provider SSOT. Importable consumers read resolved leaves at the use site; genuine non-entrypoint helpers remain out of this ticket and use ADR 0019 Â§5.5 pure-defaults / bootstrap-injection treatment.

## Problem

Three active importable consumers still re-derived config-owned path defaults:

- `ai/daemons/bridge/daemon.mjs` used `process.env.NEO_AI_DB_PATH || '.neo-ai-data/sqlite/memory-core-graph.sqlite'` and `process.env.NEO_AI_DAEMON_DIR || '.neo-ai-data/wake-daemon'`.
- `ai/services/memory-core/HealthService.mjs` used `process.env.NEO_HEARTBEAT_ALIVE_PATH || <repo>/.neo-ai-data/wake-daemon/heartbeat.alive` even though the Tier-1 leaf owns that env.
- `ai/scripts/diagnostics/analyzeNlTelemetry.mjs` used `process.env.NEO_MEMORY_DB_PATH || aiConfig.storagePaths.graph`, duplicating the Memory Core graph leaf env resolution.

These local fallback chains drift from the owning config leaves and make test-mode resolution harder to reason about. The #12464 one-line variant was rejected as negative ROI; this ticket is the clustered form that removes the same pattern across daemon, service, and diagnostic consumers with focused tests.

## Contract Ledger Matrix

| Target Surface | Source of Authority | Proposed Behavior | Fallback / Edge Case | Docs | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Bridge daemon graph DB path | ADR 0019; `ai/mcp/server/memory-core/config.template.mjs#storagePaths.graph` | Read `memoryCoreConfig.storagePaths.graph` at module setup. | Unit subprocesses running with `UNIT_TEST_MODE=true` provide `NEO_MEMORY_DB_PATH_TEST`; production env remains `NEO_MEMORY_DB_PATH` through the leaf. | PR body lists the stale alias removal. | Bridge daemon unit file validates delivery against a physical test DB. |
| Bridge daemon data dir | ADR 0019; `ai/mcp/server/memory-core/config.template.mjs#wakeDaemon.dataDir` | Read `memoryCoreConfig.wakeDaemon.dataDir`. | Existing `NEO_AI_DAEMON_DIR` env remains leaf-owned. | PR body lists the retained env leaf. | Bridge daemon unit file validates PID/log/state isolation. |
| HealthService heartbeat liveness path | ADR 0019; `ai/config.template.mjs#wakeDaemonHeartbeatAlivePath`; `SwarmHeartbeatService.heartbeatAlivePath()` precedent | Read `aiConfig.wakeDaemonHeartbeatAlivePath` and export the helper for a focused invariant. | Tests apply `NEO_HEARTBEAT_ALIVE_PATH` through `AiConfig.setEnvOverride()`; no live per-read env fallback. | JSDoc names producer/consumer shared path contract. | HealthService unit file validates helper + wake features block. |
| Diagnostic NL telemetry DB path | ADR 0019; `ai/mcp/server/memory-core/config.template.mjs#storagePaths.graph` | Read `aiConfig.storagePaths.graph`; remove direct `process.env.NEO_MEMORY_DB_PATH` fallback. | Under `UNIT_TEST_MODE=true`, tests use `NEO_MEMORY_DB_PATH_TEST`; production env remains `NEO_MEMORY_DB_PATH` through the leaf. | Inline comment names leaf-owned env. | Diagnostic unit file validates subprocess behavior + source invariant. |

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `bridge/daemon.mjs` no longer references `NEO_AI_DB_PATH` or hardcoded Memory Core graph DB fallback literals.
- [ ] `bridge/daemon.mjs` reads the wake-daemon data directory from the Memory Core config leaf, preserving `NEO_AI_DAEMON_DIR` as a leaf-owned env override.
- [ ] `HealthService.heartbeatAlivePath()` reads the resolved Tier-1 heartbeat leaf and matches the existing `SwarmHeartbeatService` producer precedent.
- [ ] `analyzeNlTelemetry.mjs` reads `aiConfig.storagePaths.graph` directly; no duplicate `process.env.NEO_MEMORY_DB_PATH ||/??` fallback remains.
- [ ] Focused unit coverage proves bridge daemon subprocess delivery, HealthService wake features behavior, and the diagnostic source invariant.
- [ ] PR body documents residual A1 surfaces that remain out of this importable-consumer slice.

## Out of Scope

- Genuine non-entrypoint / pure-defaults surfaces such as `TaskDefinitions.mjs` and its orchestrator consumers.
- Broader data-root redesign / `NEO_AI_DATA_ROOT` resurrection (#12417 is still `needs-re-triage`).
- B3 defensive-read cleanup (#12461 and its cluster PRs).
- Lifecycle-script local state files that do not yet have owning config leaves.
- Public docs, Docker, examples, and historical `resources/content/**` records.

## Related

- Parent epic: #12456
- Authority: ADR 0019 (#12457 / PR #12458)
- Correction anchor: #12464 / PR #12468 closed as negative-ROI micro-split
- Related stale broad root: #12417 (`needs-re-triage`)

### @neo-claude-opus - 2026-06-04T03:53:18Z

## A1 census â€” discovery sweep (families 1â€“2), classified

V-B-A census of hardcoded config fallbacks in active consumers (`ai/daemons`, `ai/mcp/server`, `ai/scripts`, `ai/services`; **excludes** `config.template.mjs`/`config.mjs` â€” the SSOT, now guarded by the #12474 lint). Search families 1 (`process.env.NEO_* ||`/`??`) + 2 (direct `.neo-ai-data` construction + `DEFAULT_*` constants). This is the **read-only discovery artifact** â€” the behavior changes (execution) stay for daylight + after #12472 lands.

### Class 1 â€” Data-root duplication â†’ route through `DeploymentConfig.resolveAiDataRoot()` (daemons, pre-bootstrap) or `AiConfig` (importable). The #12420 pattern at scale â€” the dominant A1 cluster:
- `ai/daemons/bridge/daemon.mjs:53,54` â€” `NEO_AI_DB_PATH` / `NEO_AI_DAEMON_DIR` `|| '.neo-ai-data/...'`
- `ai/daemons/orchestrator/daemon.mjs:36` â€” `NEO_AI_ORCHESTRATOR_DIR || '.neo-ai-data/orchestrator-daemon'`
- `ai/daemons/orchestrator/TaskDefinitions.mjs:7,8,69` â€” `DEFAULT_DB_PATH` / `DEFAULT_DATA_DIR` + chroma `--path .neo-ai-data/chroma/unified` (also the ADR Â§5.5 pure-defaults target)
- `ai/daemons/orchestrator/services/HeavyMaintenanceLeaseService.mjs:7` â€” lease-path literal
- `ai/services/memory-core/HealthService.mjs:29` â€” `NEO_HEARTBEAT_ALIVE_PATH || .../heartbeat.alive`
- `ai/scripts/maintenance/{backup,restore}.mjs` (concepts / trajectories / backups dirs) â€” **note: the same files already derive `sent-to-cull` from `mcConfig.storagePaths.graph`; the good config-derived pattern sits right beside the hardcoded one â€” mirror it**
- `ai/scripts/maintenance/{auditGraphIntegrity,purgeTestCollections,defragSQLiteDB}.mjs`, `ai/scripts/diagnostics/bootstrapCodexSandbox.mjs` â€” data-dir constructions

### Class 2 â€” Config-owned policy value with hardcoded fallback â†’ `AiConfig` / canonical env decoder:
- `ai/scripts/diagnostics/analyzeNlTelemetry.mjs:23` â€” `process.env.NEO_MEMORY_DB_PATH || aiConfig.storagePaths.graph` is a **redundant** env-fallback (the `graph` leaf's env arg *is* `NEO_MEMORY_DB_PATH`, so it already resolves it). Drop the `process.env... ||`. *(This is the #12464 case â€” re-triaged under #12438's scope, where it belongs.)*
- `ai/scripts/lifecycle/checkAllAgentIdle.mjs:59` â€” `NEO_TRIO_IDENTITIES || '@neo-gemini-3-1-pro,@neo-opus-4-7,@neo-gpt'` (hardcoded swarm roster)
- `ai/services/knowledge-base/HealthService.mjs:159` â€” `NEO_OPENAI_COMPATIBLE_HOST || ...` provider host

### Class 3 â€” Legitimate local primitives â†’ KEEP (the load-bearing "do NOT touch" set â€” bounds the work):
- Runtime/request identity: `NEO_AGENT_ID || process.env.USER || 'unknown'`, `NEO_SESSION_ID || null`, `NEO_AGENT_IDENTITY || '@system'` (`knowledge-base/Server.mjs:106,110`, `toolService.mjs:79,80`, `KBRecorderService.mjs:220`, `trioWakeCooldown.mjs:63`, `idleOutNudge.mjs:120`) â€” per-request identity, not config.
- No-op fallbacks: `|| undefined` (`syncKnowledgeBase.mjs:20`), `|| null` (`bootstrapWorktree.mjs:930`).
- Local algorithm-tuning constants (not config-owned policy): `DEFAULT_SAFE_FRACTION`, `DEFAULT_POLL_INTERVAL_MS`, `DEFAULT_SAFETY_MARGIN_ROWS`, `DEFAULT_BATCH_SIZE`, `DEFAULT_{ACTIVE,IDLE}_WINDOW_MS`, `DEFAULT_HEAVY_MAINTENANCE_*`, the `DEFAULT_*_TASK_NAMES` lists.
- `.neo-ai-data` inside ignore/skip lists (`FileSystemIngestor.mjs:31`, `WakeDecisionService.mjs:33`) â€” literals, not path construction.
- Secret-env reads (GEMINI / GH_TOKEN / `NEO_KB_INGEST_TOKEN`) â€” explicitly out of scope.

### Class 4 â€” Borderline (per-case judgment at execution):
- Hardcoded agent-identity defaults (`NEO_AGENT_IDENTITY || '@neo-gemini-3-1-pro'`, `checkSunsetted.mjs:79,242`) â€” smelly but not config-owned policy.
- `toolService.mjs:20` openapi path (co-located default).
- Migration-script chroma host/port (`backfillChromaSharedUserId.mjs:80,81`) â€” one-off scripts may justify standalone.
- Several `DEFAULT_*` tuning constants that *could* be operator knobs â€” needs the #11828 "is this operator policy?" call, per-constant.

### Coverage + next
- **Covered:** families 1 (`NEO_* ||`/`??`) + 2 (`.neo-ai-data` construction + `DEFAULT_*`).
- **Partial:** a dedicated per-`DEFAULT_*` operator-policy judgment pass (Class 4) remains.
- **Execution is daylight-deferred:** behavior changes to production config consumers need careful per-instance judgment, and any folded-in config leaves should land after the #12474 lint (#12472) merges. **The Class-1 daemon data-root cluster is the highest-value, most-mechanical starting point** â€” `resolveAiDataRoot` already exists as the target helper.

â€” neo-claude-opus (/lead-role); discovery sweep only. Execution lane open for daylight pickup (me next session, or a peer).
